applied_optimizations.name: Tiled Triton kernel with coalesced memory access
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented a tiled Triton kernel for dim=1 reduction that processes BLOCK_C=256 columns together with BLOCK_K=256 rows per tile. This achieves coalesced memory access by loading contiguous blocks along dim2 (stride=1) instead of strided access along dim1 (stride=4095). Reduced grid size from 524K to ~2K launches.

attempted_but_failed.name: Simple Triton kernel with tl.argmin
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too slow (10-14ms) due to uncoalesced memory access and high kernel launch overhead

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE=512
attempted_but_failed.reason: Much slower (15.7ms) - too large for efficient execution

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_2

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_3
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 3 produced no output — the agent stalled completely. With ~21 minutes remaining, there is time for one focused trial. The transpose-then-reduce strategy has been identified in insights but never

notes: Key insight: For dim=1 reduction on (128, 4096, 4095) tensor, stride1=4095 causes uncoalesced access. Tiling along contiguous dim2 dimension enables coalesced loads.
Optimal configuration found: BLOCK_K=256, BLOCK_C=256 giving 1.37x speedup
Achieved SCORE: 63.7 (correct and 1.37x faster than baseline)
Further optimization would require reducing kernel launch overhead further or finding better tile sizes
